title: ATER: Tariffs and Weak Demand Drove Revenue and Margin Declines
text: Aterian ATER Revenue Decline and Headwinds Analysis. Q3 2025 Performance: Net revenue decreased 27.5% to $19 million, attributed to lower consumer demand following tariff-related price increases. Net revenue was $19 million, a significant decline from Q3 2024; however, it represented just a 2% decrease from the previous quarter. Key Headwinds: Tariff Impact: Revenue and margins declined sharply year-over-year due to tariffs, macroeconomic headwinds, and softer consumer demand. These US policies made it difficult to navigate considering the speed they were implemented in and the magnitude of the tariffs themselves. Consumer Demand Softness: A general slowdown in consumer spending contributed to the decline in revenue. Reduced overall consumer demand was likely due in part to uncertainty surrounding tariff trade policy, pricing pressures, softer market conditions, or a shift in discretionary spending. Margin Compression: Gross margin declined to 56.1% from 60.3%, primarily due to product mix, tariff impacts, and a $400,000 product remediation charge. Company Response: Despite the challenges, management attributed sharp year-over-year declines in revenue and gross margin to tariff-related pricing actions and broader demand softness, but reported that key profitability metrics improved sequentially through aggressive cost containment. The company suspended its share repurchase program to preserve capital as it navigates tariff impacts, competitive pressures, and market diversification efforts.
